name: Cornucopia
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Lightly spray a cookie sheet, at least 17" x 14", with non-stick cooking spray.
2. Tear off a 30" x 18-inch sheet of heavy duty aluminum foil. Fold in half to form a rectangle measuring 18" x 15". Roll diagonally to form a hollow cone about 18 inches long with a diameter of 5 inches at the widest end (Cornucopia opening). Secure the end with clear tape. Stuff the cone with crumpled regular foil until rigid. Spray the outside of the cone with non-stick cooking spray. Place on the cookie sheet.
3. Unroll the first can of breadstick dough on a work surface. Separate the breadsticks. Begin by wrapping one breadstick around the tip of the cone. Brush the end of the next breadstick with the egg wash and press to attach to the end of the first breadstick. Continue spiral-wrapping the cone, slightly overlapping the dough until three breadsticks remain.
4. Pinch one end of the remaining three breadsticks together, then braid them. Brush the exterior of the Cornucopia opening with the egg wash. Gently press the braid into place. Brush the entire Cornucopia with the egg wash.
5. Bake for 45 minutes in the preheated oven or until the bread is a rich brown color. If parts start to darken too much, cover them with pieces of foil.
6. Remove from the oven and let cool completely on the cookie sheet on a wire rack. Carefully remove the tape when cooled. (If freezing, leave the tape in place for support; remove it when thawed.)
7. Fill the Cornucopia with assorted raw vegetables directly on the table so they spill out of the opening.
NOTE: To prevent the center-piece from absorbing atmospheric moisture, the baked Cornucopia cone can be sprayed with shellac or clear enamel. If treated in this manner, the Cornucopia will become inedible but can be preserved and reused.
